summaries.content: This lesson introduces the poem 'Naav Banao Naav Banao' (नाव बनाओ नाव बनाओ) from the Rimjhim textbook. The poem is a playful, rhythmic piece about the joy of making paper boats. It captures the excitement of children who fold paper into boat shapes and float them in the rainwater that flows through streets and lanes. The repetition of 'नाव बनाओ' (Make a boat!) creates an energetic, song-like rhythm that invites children to join in the fun. The poem celebrates the simple pleasures of childhood and the magic of turning a plain sheet of paper into an adventurous little vessel.
keyPoints: The poem is about making paper boats (कागज़ की नाव) and floating them in rainwater
Repetition of 'नाव बनाओ' creates an energetic, musical rhythm
It captures the simple joy and excitement of childhood play
The poem is from the Rimjhim textbook for Class 4 Hindi

summaries.content: This lesson explores the imagery and imagination in the poem. Once the children make their paper boats and set them afloat, the poem describes the boat's journey through flowing water. The wind (हवा) pushes the boat forward, the water (पानी) carries it along, and in the children's imagination, the tiny boat travels to faraway lands. The poem uses beautiful descriptive language to paint pictures of rain (बारिश), flowing streams (धारा), and the excitement of watching a handmade creation sail away. Students learn how poetry uses imagination (कल्पना) to transform simple activities into magical adventures.
keyPoints: The poem describes the paper boat's journey through rainwater streams
Wind and water work together to carry the boat on its adventure
Children use imagination (कल्पना) to see the boat traveling to distant places
The poem uses descriptive language to create vivid mental pictures

summaries.content: This lesson focuses on the language and poetic elements of the poem. Students learn about rhyming (तुकबंदी) - how matching sounds at the end of lines make the poem musical. They study the rhythm (लय) created by the repeated pattern 'नाव बनाओ, नाव बनाओ'. The lesson introduces vocabulary related to rain and water: बारिश (rain), पानी (water), धारा (stream), तैरना (to float), बहना (to flow). Students also learn about imperative verbs (आज्ञा वाचक) like बनाओ (make!), तैराओ (float!), and चलाओ (sail!).
keyPoints: Rhyming (तुकबंदी) makes the poem musical and easy to remember
Repetition of 'नाव बनाओ' creates a strong, catchy rhythm
New vocabulary: बारिश, पानी, धारा, तैरना, बहना, हवा
Imperative verbs (आज्ञा वाचक): बनाओ, तैराओ, चलाओ

summaries.content: This lesson covers comprehension activities and creative exercises based on the poem. Students answer questions about the poem, practise reciting it with proper rhythm and expression, and try writing their own short poems about rainy-day activities. The hands-on activity involves actually making paper boats and floating them, connecting the poem to real experience. Students also discuss their favourite rainy-day memories and activities, practising speaking in Hindi. The lesson concludes with a discussion about how poetry captures feelings and experiences in a beautiful, musical way.
keyPoints: Students practise reciting the poem with rhythm and expression
Hands-on activity: making real paper boats and floating them
Creative writing: students write their own short poems about rain
Discussion about how poetry captures feelings in a musical way
